name: Parish Church
unlockCondition: 10,000 ducats, Church approval, local community support
shortDescription: Neighborhood worship center serving the spiritual needs of local residents.
fullDescription: The spiritual heart of each Venetian neighborhood, parish churches provide religious services, community gathering spaces, and social support. Featuring a main worship area, bell tower, side chapels, and often charitable facilities, these buildings serve as both religious centers and neighborhood focal points. Venice's distinctive layout resulted in an unusually high number of parish churches, each forming the center of a local community.
flavorText: The parish church marks both the geographical center of the neighborhood and the spiritual center of its people's lives—from baptism to burial, all significant moments pass through these doors.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Central location in neighborhood, often facing campo
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates community focal point, bell sounds at regular intervals

upgradePath: Expanded Parish Church with additional chapels and services

soundDesign.activitySounds: Choir practice, Mass ceremonies, community gatherings

seasonalEffects.ordinary time: Regular rhythm of daily Masses and prayers

name: Chapel
shortDescription: Small private worship space often attached to palaces or institutions.
fullDescription: Meeting the spiritual needs of private households or institutions, chapels provide dedicated worship spaces on a smaller scale than parish churches. Featuring an altar, devotional artwork, and often family memorials, these intimate religious spaces allow for private worship while demonstrating the owner's piety and status. Many Venetian noble families maintained private chapels within their palazzos or funded chapels within larger churches.
flavorText: The lord of the house may command all who dwell beneath his roof—save in the chapel, where all kneel equally before a higher authority.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Often within private palazzos or institutions
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Connection to main building, some privacy
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates sense of sanctity, slight incense aroma

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian chapel under construction, small private religious space, fine stonework being installed with particular attention to detail, small altar area taking shape, space for devotional artwork being prepared, high-quality but intimate proportions, specialized craftsmen working on religious elements, private funding evident in quality of materials, ornate ceiling work in progress, space for family memorials or crypts, integration with connected palazzo or institution, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, sense of private devotion balanced with status display

seasonalEffects.family events: Specially decorated for weddings, funerals, baptisms

name: Town Hall
unlockCondition: 12,000 ducats, local government appointment or connection
shortDescription: Local government offices providing administrative services for a district.
fullDescription: Handling local governance needs, town halls serve as administrative centers for Venice's districts. Featuring offices for district officials, public meeting spaces, record archives, and often small courtrooms, these buildings manage the day-to-day governance tasks below the level of the central government. Their accessible location and public service focus made them important neighborhood institutions.
flavorText: Between the lofty politics of the Senate and the daily concerns of the citizen lies the town hall—where grand policies become practical solutions to everyday problems.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Central district location, often on secondary campo
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases civic activity, creates administrative center

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian town hall under construction, district government building, substantial civic structure taking shape, administrative offices being partitioned, public meeting hall with raised dais for officials, document storage vaults with security features, district symbols being carved into facade, official architectural style following government regulations, quality construction showing civic importance, public counter areas for citizen services, separate entrance for officials, dedicated clerk spaces, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, balance of authority and accessibility in design

soundDesign.ambientSound: Quill scratching, official stamps, muted conversations
soundDesign.activitySounds: Public announcements, dispute resolutions, citizen petitions

name: Courthouse
unlockCondition: 20,000 ducats, judicial appointment or connection, government approval
shortDescription: Judicial facility for legal proceedings, records, and administration of justice.
fullDescription: The physical embodiment of Venetian justice, courthouses provide spaces for legal proceedings from simple disputes to complex trials. Featuring multiple courtrooms, judicial offices, legal archives, and holding cells, these imposing buildings represent the rule of law in the Republic. Venice's sophisticated legal system required substantial facilities to manage its complex commercial and criminal jurisprudence.
flavorText: Between the stones of this building lie not just mortar, but the accumulated wisdom of centuries of law—for Venice conquers by trade, but endures by justice.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Government district, near other official buildings
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases order in surrounding area, creates serious atmosphere

soundDesign.ambientSound: Echoing halls, distant proceedings, hushed conversations
soundDesign.activitySounds: Gavel strikes, oath-taking, verdict announcements, case arguments

name: Public Archives
shortDescription: Document storage and records facility preserving governmental and commercial information.
fullDescription: Preserving the documentation that powers Venetian governance and commerce, archives securely store and organize vital records. Featuring specialized storage systems, reading rooms, copying facilities, and often restoration workshops, these buildings protect everything from property deeds to diplomatic treaties. Venice's sophisticated bureaucracy generated enormous volumes of documents requiring careful preservation and retrieval systems.
flavorText: The true power of Venice lies not in her ships or gold, but in these carefully preserved papers—for they contain the accumulated knowledge of centuries of statecraft and commerce.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Government district, often near administrative centers
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates scholarly atmosphere, increases official activity

name: Navigation School
shortDescription: Specialized educational facility teaching maritime skills, navigation, and shiphandling.
fullDescription: Training the sailors who power Venice's maritime empire, navigation schools teach crucial skills from basic seamanship to advanced celestial navigation. Featuring classrooms, navigation equipment, chart rooms, model ships, and often small observatories, these institutions ensure the continued excellence of Venetian maritime capabilities. Their specialized curriculum combines theoretical knowledge with practical experience.
flavorText: A ship without a navigator is merely driftwood with pretensions—here we transform ordinary men into masters of the sea itself.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Maritime districts, preferably with water view
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Access to docks for practical training
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases maritime competence in area, attracts sailors

soundDesign.ambientSound: Classroom discussions, chart paper rustling, instruments being demonstrated
soundDesign.activitySounds: Navigation calculations, instructor lectures, student questions

name: Art Academy
unlockCondition: 15,000 ducats, Artistic Guild connection, cultural reputation
shortDescription: Educational institution teaching painting, sculpture, and other fine arts to students.
fullDescription: Nurturing the artistic talent that adorns Venice's buildings and collections, art academies provide formal training in various creative disciplines. Featuring studios with specialized lighting, sculpture workshops, model rooms, exhibition spaces, and often material preparation areas, these institutions combine technical instruction with artistic philosophy. Venice's distinctive artistic style developed within these academies.
flavorText: Talent without training is like a ship without a rudder—here we provide not just techniques, but the wisdom to know when to follow tradition and when to challenge it.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Cultural districts, often near churches or palaces
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Northern light exposure preferred for studios
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases cultural prestige, artistic activity in area

name: Hospital
unlockCondition: 20,000 ducats, Medical Guild connection, government approval
shortDescription: Medical care facility providing treatment and recovery services to the ill and injured.
fullDescription: Providing essential healthcare to Venice's population, hospitals combine medical treatment, convalescence, and often charity for the poor. Featuring treatment rooms, patient wards, apothecary stations, surgical areas, and isolation facilities, these large institutions represent significant civic investment in public health. Venice's position as a trading hub made medical facilities particularly important for managing diseases from abroad.
flavorText: Between the physician's knowledge and God's mercy lies the hospital—where both science and faith work together to preserve Venice's most precious resource: her people.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Some distance from dense residential, fresh air access
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Water access for cleanliness, garden space
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Improves health in surrounding area, slight medical aromas

name: Quarantine Station
unlockCondition: 15,000 ducats, government authorization, Health Office approval
shortDescription: Specialized facility for isolating potentially contagious travelers and goods before entry into Venice.
fullDescription: Protecting Venice from imported diseases, quarantine stations isolate ships, travelers, and goods for observation before permitting city entry. Featuring separated accommodation blocks, inspection areas, fumigation facilities, and secure boundaries, these complex facilities implement Venice's pioneering public health measures. The term 'quarantine' itself originates from Venice's forty-day isolation period (quaranta giorni).
flavorText: Forty days may seem a harsh price for entry to our city, but consider the alternative: the finest goods and richest merchants mean nothing if they bring the Black Death as an uninvited companion.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Isolated location, often on outer island or lagoon edge
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Water access for ships, security perimeter
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Prevents disease spread, creates controlled isolation area

soundDesign.ambientSound: Distant ship horns, water sounds, seagulls, isolation
soundDesign.activitySounds: Health inspections, fumigation processes, ships docking

name: Theater
unlockCondition: 12,000 ducats, Cultural Guild connection, noble patronage
shortDescription: Entertainment venue for performances, including plays, music, and commedia dell'arte.
fullDescription: Enriching Venetian cultural life, theaters provide venues for various performances from sophisticated dramas to popular entertainment. Featuring a stage, audience seating often in multiple tiers, backstage facilities, and sometimes refreshment areas, these buildings serve as important social gathering spaces. Venice's theater tradition blended intellectual content with spectacular staging and music.
flavorText: On this stage we hold a mirror to Venice herself—sometimes flattering, sometimes critical, but always revealing truths that might otherwise remain unspoken.

locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases cultural activity, creates evening entertainment hub

name: Public Bath
shortDescription: Hygiene facility providing bathing services to urban residents without private facilities.
fullDescription: Supporting public health through cleanliness, bath houses provide washing facilities for citizens without private baths. Featuring bathing pools of varying temperatures, steam rooms, changing areas, and often medicinal services, these establishments combine practical hygiene with social gathering. Venice's water-surrounded situation made public baths particularly important for controlling disease and maintaining cleanliness.
flavorText: Cleanliness of body promotes health of body, and the shared conversation of the baths promotes health of mind—thus a city with good bath houses benefits twice over.

locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Improves public health, creates social gathering point
